Looking at this formula, I need to carefully preserve its logical structure while making it more readable.

The formula has the form: (a & A) | (!a & B), where:
- A = ((a & (b U c)) | (!a & (!c U ((!b & !c) | G!c))))
- B = ((!a & (b U c)) | (a & (!c U ((!b & !c) | G!c))))

Let me rewrite this systematically:

Either a is true and one of the following holds:
- a is true and b remains true until c becomes true, or
- a is false and c remains false until either both b and c are false or c remains false forever;

or a is false and one of the following holds:
- a is false and b remains true until c becomes true, or
- a is true and c remains false until either both b and c are false or c remains false forever.
